name: Guptos
message: The extension's impressive and very handy overall, can you add shortcuts to pin and close the windows too? That'd be great.
Hello, thank you for this wonderful extension!  Please fix the issue where the window size minimizes.

name: Projekta2
message: This matches something I've noticed building a tool around idle tab behavior: the visual feedback loop is doing more work than people realize. When tabs look "organized" (grouped, collapsed, whatever), the discomfort that used to make you close things disappears — but the underlying cost (RAM, attention, whatever metric you care about) doesn't move at all.  What's interesting is that this isn't really about tab groups specifically — it's that any UI that hides quantity without reducing it removes the feedback signal that drove the original behavior change. Same pattern shows up in inbox folders, Slack channel muting, anything where "out of sight" gets mistaken for "resolved."  The fix that's actually worked for people I've talked to isn't a better organization system, it's reintroducing a cost signal — something that makes the accumulation visible again instead of invisible.
